behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on a real vision model correctly classifying explicit content; a mocked classifier can trivially pass while missing real misclassification behavior. The hold/not-publish path should be asserted end-to-end with model output.
behaviors.test_description: Submit known explicit test images through the real moderation endpoint with the real vision model enabled, then verify each post is persisted in a held-for-review state and never reaches the published feed.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model-quality behavior under adversarial text variants. Mocked text classifiers hide the exact failure mode (missed obfuscated hate speech), so real model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Send a corpus of hate-speech phrases with obfuscations (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks) through the real text moderation path and assert they are flagged and held.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is local handoff/state wiring between moderation results and publish decision (handler/service/repo/queue). Real model intelligence is not required if inputs are clearly safe; a unit test could miss cross-module drop/hold bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the post-ingest flow with both moderation components returning 'safe' and assert the record transitions to published state and appears in retrieval queries, with no lingering held/queued status.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLO across multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision + text moderation) plus decisioning. Mocked models or isolated components hide real latency composition and queueing effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions against the production-like pipeline using real model calls for both modalities, measure submission-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 <= 5s under peak-hour representative load.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Failure handling depends on real external-call failure modes (timeouts/5xx/connection errors) and queue behavior. In-process mocks can miss retry-trigger conditions and transport-level error handling.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real HTTP unavailability for the vision dependency (e.g., fault-injected endpoint/timeouts), submit image posts, and verify they are enqueued with retry metadata and never auto-published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Borderline consistency is driven by real model variability and any stabilization logic (e.g., caching/windowed dedupe). A deterministic mock cannot expose nondeterministic classification drift.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the configured short window using the real vision model path, then assert decision consistency policy is met (identical final moderation state across repeats).
